frames.name: Economic Opportunity Frame
frames.description: Presents the proposal as expanding access to stock market gains and addressing workplace inequality
frames.emphasis: Stock market benefits, addressing disparity between workers with and without employer plans, economic empowerment

frames.name: Policy Mechanics Frame
frames.description: Focuses on technical implementation details and existing legislative framework
frames.emphasis: How the plan would work, connection to SECURE 2.0 Act, TSP model, tax implications

frames.name: Bipartisan Policy Solution Frame
frames.description: Emphasizes cross-party support and collaboration on retirement security
frames.emphasis: Bipartisan interest in expanding retirement access, collaboration between progressive and conservative economists

frames.name: Private-Public Partnership Frame
frames.description: Highlights role of philanthropic funding alongside government matching
frames.emphasis: Private investor contributions, comparison to other Trump initiatives funded by donations

outlierPerspectives: Social Security trust fund depletion as competing priority (Axios only)
Detailed funding mechanism through existing Savers Match program (Daily Wire only)
Specific mention of Michael and Susan Dell's $6.25 billion contribution to Trump kids accounts (Daily Wire only)
Quote from Teresa Ghilarducci about bipartisan collaboration with Kevin Hassett in 2021 (Axios only)
missingContext: Cost estimates and budget impact of the proposal
Comparison to existing state auto-IRA programs
Opposition viewpoints or criticism from policy experts
Details on how private contributions would be regulated
Impact on existing retirement savings programs
